item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: seeing MC&D owning the foundation is a rare treat.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP is an MC&D SCP. Therefore, I propose a link to the MC&D hub.

Editing this:

the Foundation may not acquire any instances of SCP-3303 except by purchasing it from Marshall, Carter, and Dark.

To this:

the Foundation may not acquire any instances of SCP-3303 except by purchasing it from Marshall, Carter, and Dark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Slowly walkin' down the hall, faster than a cannonball.

The name reference works, I guess. Champagne Supernova by Oasis, if you don't know.

A neat concept, but the reason for the human jawbone went over my head and seemed completely out of the left field. I'd like to know what it was about, if it is related somehow, but whatever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just anybody who observes it. I changed the wording a lil bit to make that clearer.

The SBR reference is in the fact that MC&D used a mummified human jawbone to do all of this — a Saint Corpse Part, essentially.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this. Does "subjects" (re: subjects perceiving an aura) refer to those consuming the champagne specifically?

Also, I'm somehow missing the SBR reference… is it the time dilation itself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed reading the draft of this. I enjoyed it even more on the site. Have a boat good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I never had the Foundation make any assumptions about what was responsible for the anomaly. That's fanon, not canon.

What, exactly, should the Foundation have asked of MC&D here? From the perspective, of course, of them not knowing a single thing about what makes this Champagne anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the Foundation discovered this party-enhancing wine, found out MC&D was involved somehow, and just immediately presumed that the vineyard itself was the source of the anomaly — rather than the GoI that is specifically known for their uber-rich party-enhancing anomalies?

I don't think the Foundation got hosed, here. I think whoever was calling the shots was just a moron. Which is fine, I mean — sometimes, organizations make really boneheaded decisions!

I think, though, this would work better if there was some sort of indication that MC&D fooled the Foundation into thinking this was a thing they had stumbled across and discovered themselves, rather than a thing they were, in fact, causing? Mention falsified historical accounts of previous anomalies at the vineyard, maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I rarely have the patience to write long articles. 3003 took me weeks, even after I'd been brainstorming about it on and off for years.

When writing this, I had two choices if I wanted to expand it: show the champagne working without a hitch, or show it having a problem. The first seemed boring and unnecessary — if I had to spell out all the things people could do with a never-ending party, it's probably because I didn't establish the premise well enough to let people imagine it. The second felt unrealistic and kind of cliche — why would MC&D risk losing clients and reputation by putting out a malfunctioning product, and why can nothing in the Foundationverse work as advertised?

Ultimately, I felt like focusing too much on the effects was a dead end narratively, so I redirected my efforts to telling a story about MC&D and the Foundation, using the champagne as a reference point. And, well, that one just didn't take many words to write.

If it makes you feel better, with this and SCP-3113 and maybe others, I'm laying the groundwork for a story. I don't know what that story is, or if I'll ever write it, but this might not have to stand on its own forever.

And MC&D, knowing that they could use the jawbone to move and set up shop somewhere else, didn't feel like bribing the Foundation enough to look the other way about this stuff indefinitely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels very bare-bones, though the very final paragraph suggests a whole lot. Champagne that makes the evening last forever is a very MC&D product, though. I just wonder why they stopped producing it…

item.title: bataille tendency
item.content:encoded: Solid. Making a way for time to not fly when you're having fun is absolutely a thing MC&D would do and the god damn Steel Ball Run reference pisses me off in the good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with all of this and above. I too have this weird love of seeing the Foundation get destroyed at their own game, and this is a perfect example of such. Upvoted.

item.title: Re: Hawly shiet!! Is dat a motherfucking JawJouh reference???
(no title)
item.content:encoded: Temporal anomalies, amoral capitalist bastards, chumping the Foundation. There's a lot here to like. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*snerk* This is good. I love Foundation-GoI interactions like this, and I generally like MC&D. I don't get any references you may have put in here, but this is a good skip.

You actually made me upvote something where the Foundation gets played, damn you.

item.title: Holy shit!! Is that a motherfucking JoJo reference???
item.content:encoded:
<kinch> this fucking rules
<kinch> they got hosed
<kinch> the foundation got hosed
<kinch> they got owned
<kinch> the scp is this fucked up jawbone
<kinch> this is just like scp-@-a

enjoy my scp you nerd bastards

EDIT: kinch you're my mortal enemy i'm going to break into your house and destroy all of your video games

EDIT EDIT: forgot to say that Malcious deserves some credit for this — I'd been thinking to myself "I don't have any MC&D SCPs and I wanna change that, what should I write about?) and then I refreshed Recent Forum Posts and this was at the top. So I just ran with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On an elemental level, I love it when the Foundation gets owned. Even if it's the shithead capitalists do it, I love it. The anomaly itself is interesting and puzzling in just the right way. And if you're familiar with Steel Ball Run, it just pisses you off in that good way.
